description: Test cases for the Spec-to-Acceptance-Criteria Auditor skill

evals.prompt:
Can you review this PRD before I send it to engineering?

---
# Feature: User Profile Editing

## Overview
Users should be able to edit their profile. This will improve user experience and make the product feel more polished.

## What We're Building
A profile editing page where users can update their information.

## Requirements
- Users can edit their profile
- The page should look good
- Changes should be saved
- It should work on mobile
- Show errors if something goes wrong
- Users with appropriate permissions can edit

## Success
Users will love being able to edit their profiles and it should increase engagement.
---
evals.expected_output: A full 5-pass audit report identifying: (1) missing problem statement tied to user need, (2) no measurable success metrics, (3) no out-of-scope definition, (4) multiple untestable ACs ('look good', 'work on mobile', 'something goes wrong', 'appropriate permissions'), (5) ambiguous 'it' pronouns, (6) scope risks like 'work on mobile' and 'appropriate permissions', (7) sprint readiness verdict of Not Ready with multiple blockers. Should include specific rewrites for each untestable AC.

evals.prompt:
Is this spec ready to go into the sprint? We're planning to start Monday.

---
# Search Autocomplete Feature

## Problem Statement
Users frequently abandon search when they don't see immediate results. Support tickets show 'search doesn't work' is the #1 complaint (340 tickets in Q3). We believe showing autocomplete suggestions will reduce abandonment and help users find what they're looking for.

## Success Metrics
- Reduce search abandonment rate from 42% to below 30% (measured 30 days post-launch)
- P95 autocomplete suggestion latency < 150ms
- Track via existing analytics events + new autocomplete_selected event

## Out of Scope (v1)
- Fuzzy matching / typo correction
- Personalized suggestions based on user history
- Search within categories (search is global only)

## Acceptance Criteria
1. As the user types in the search box, suggestions appear after 2 characters are entered
2. Up to 5 suggestions are shown
3. Suggestions match the beginning of the query string against product names and categories
4. Selecting a suggestion navigates the user to the search results page for that query
5. Pressing Escape dismisses the suggestion dropdown
6. The dropdown is accessible (keyboard navigable, screen reader compatible)
7. Suggestions load quickly

## Error States
- If the autocomplete service is unavailable, the search box functions normally without suggestions (graceful degradation)
- If the query returns 0 suggestions, no dropdown appears

## Dependencies
- Backend autocomplete API endpoint (owned by Search team, ETA: week of Nov 4)
- Design file: [link placeholder]

## Rollback
- Feature flag: autocomplete_enabled (default: off, rolling out to 10% → 50% → 100%)
---
evals.expected_output: Mostly positive audit with sprint readiness of 'Ready with fixes'. Should identify: (1) AC #6 'accessible' needs WCAG level specified (Warning), (2) AC #7 'load quickly' is untestable — needs specific latency threshold (Warning, should note that P95 <150ms is already in metrics so this can be referenced), (3) 'keyboard navigable' needs specifics (tab order, arrow keys, Enter to select), (4) design file link is a placeholder (Note), (5) no definition of what 'beginning of query string' means for multi-word queries. Should flag that the spec is substantially strong and most findings are Warnings/Notes, not blockers.

evals.prompt:
Take a look at these user stories for our new billing feature before we start estimating:

---
# Billing Portal - User Stories

## Story 1: View Current Plan
As a user, I want to see my current plan so that I know what I'm paying for.

Acceptance Criteria:
- User can see their current plan name and price
- User can see their next billing date
- User can see their payment method on file
- The page loads appropriately fast

## Story 2: Upgrade Plan
As a user, I want to upgrade my plan so that I can access more features.

Acceptance Criteria:
- User can see available plans to upgrade to
- User can select a new plan
- User is charged appropriately
- User receives confirmation
- Handle all payment errors gracefully

## Story 3: Cancel Subscription
As a user, I want to cancel my subscription so that I stop being charged.

Acceptance Criteria:
- User can cancel from the billing page
- Cancellation takes effect at the appropriate time
- User receives confirmation email
- User can still access the product for the remainder of their billing period
---
evals.expected_output: This is a high-risk feature (payments) so several Warnings should escalate to Blockers. Key findings: (1) 'charged appropriately' in Story 2 is a Blocker — proration logic undefined, (2) 'handle all payment errors gracefully' is a Blocker — specific error states required for payments, (3) 'appropriately fast' in Story 1 is untestable, (4) 'cancellation takes effect at the appropriate time' is a Blocker — end-of-period vs. immediate is a critical business rule, (5) no rollback/kill switch defined (Blocker for payment feature), (6) no dependency on payment processor defined (Stripe, etc.), (7) 'confirmation' in Story 2 needs specifics (email? in-app? both?). Sprint readiness: Not Ready with multiple blockers.

evals.prompt:
Quick gut check on this — does this have everything it needs?

---
# Notification Preferences

Let users control which notifications they receive.

Users can turn off any notification type. Settings are saved immediately. Changes apply to future notifications only, not ones already in the queue.

The following notification types are configurable:
- New comment on your post (email + in-app)
- Someone follows you (email only)
- Weekly digest (email only)
- Product updates from us (email only)

Each type has an on/off toggle. Email and in-app channels are configured separately where both exist.

Edge cases:
- If a user turns off all email notifications, they still receive transactional emails (password reset, billing receipts)
- Users cannot turn off billing-related notifications

We'll measure success by a reduction in unsubscribe rate from our email list (currently 0.8%/month, target: below 0.5%).
---
evals.expected_output: This is a relatively strong spec. Should identify: (1) no formal AC structure — requirements are embedded in prose (Warning), (2) no error states for save failures (Warning), (3) 'saved immediately' — is this optimistic UI with background save, or blocking? (Warning), (4) 'changes apply to future notifications only, not ones already in the queue' — what defines 'in the queue'? (Note), (5) no out-of-scope section — mobile push notifications, SMS? (Note), (6) no actor definition — is this for all user types or specific roles? (Note), (7) success metric is good but no measurement window specified. Sprint readiness: Ready with fixes — the spec is largely complete but needs AC formalization.
